# Fuel Path Fleet-Lite Route Savings Report

Report date: 2026-07-02
Operator segment: Sample recurring-route operator
Prepared for: Validation buyer sample
Prepared by: Fuel Path
Evidence status: validation sample, not a guaranteed savings claim

## Summary

Decision: Sample only. Not commercial proof yet.

Main opportunity: show the buyer which recurring routes have useful net fuel stops after their selected discount or policy rule is applied.

Main blocker: provider terms, buyer discount eligibility and live acceptance evidence must be confirmed before this becomes a sales claim.

Recommended next step: replace these sample rows with 5-10 real recurring routes supplied by one pilot operator.

## Assumptions

| Assumption | Value | Evidence status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Fuel type | Mixed petrol/diesel | Sample |
| Vehicle or average economy | Not modelled in this sample | Assumption |
| Approved brands | Buyer supplied | Required |
| Fuel card or discount rule | Selected eligibility only | Required |
| Regions covered | Cross-jurisdictional routes supported where providers allow | Provider evidence required |
| Report period | One validation sample | Validation sample |

## Route Review

| Route | Fuel | Recommended stop | Pump price | Adjusted price | Best price by | Detour | Decision | Limitation |
| --- | --- | --- | ---: | ---: | ---: | ---: | --- | --- |
| Depot to Northside job | U91 | Ampol Example West | 191.9 | 187.9 | 6.2 | 2.1 min | Use when already passing | Provider terms and live acceptance need buyer validation |
| Depot to airport run | DL | Caltex Example East | 199.5 | 195.5 | 4.1 | 0.8 min | Likely worthwhile | Fleet-card eligibility not yet confirmed |
| South warehouse to CBD | U91 | 7-Eleven Example Central | 194.7 | 190.7 | 3.6 | 1.4 min | Watch route | Discount assumes selected eligibility |
| Regional supplier loop | P95 | BP Example Regional | 214.9 | 214.9 | 0.0 | 0.2 min | Pump price only | Cycle intelligence not back-tested |
| Home base to school route | E10 | Independent Example | 186.9 | 186.9 | 2.8 | 3.0 min | Driver choice | Availability at arrival not proven |

## What This Suggests

- Fuel Path should sell the route decision layer, not generic fuel-map parity.
- The strongest B2B wedge is repeat routes where eligibility, policy and detour rules change the answer.
- A buyer-facing report is viable once real route runs can be exported with provider confidence and discount evidence.

## What This Does Not Prove

- It does not guarantee total dollar savings.
- It does not prove station availability at arrival time.
- It does not confirm fleet-card acceptance unless the buyer or provider evidence confirms it.
- It does not replace driver judgement, safety, route constraints or company policy.
